Based on the context provided, specifically the section on the **Midlothian campaign**, William Ewart Gladstone gave a series of speeches in Scotland (Midlothian) in 1878–80. The text explicitly states that at that time, he was the "leader of Britain's **Liberal Party**." This campaign set the stage for his comeback and his subsequent victory in the 1880 general election, where he led the Liberal Party to power.

**Answer:** The Liberal Party
